expectations.text: Defines who the AI is with role, domain, tone, and audience
expectations.evidence: Layer 1 Identity: SupportAgent role, SaaS domain, professional/warm/concise tone, existing customers audience

expectations.text: Describes the environment, tools, and knowledge sources available
expectations.evidence: Layer 2 Context: knowledge_base delimiter, available tools, escalation paths, product scope with plan tiers

expectations.text: Contains numbered hard rules including feature fabrication and refund prohibitions
expectations.evidence: Layer 3 Constraints: 7 numbered NEVER rules including constraint 1 (fabrication) and constraint 2 (refunds)

expectations.text: Specifies how the AI should format its responses
expectations.evidence: Layer 4 Output Format: conversational structure (Greet-Acknowledge-Resolve-Escalate-Confirm), escalation message format, 200-word limit

expectations.text: The refund constraint covers paraphrases like credit, compensation, making it right
expectations.evidence: Constraint 2 explicitly covers: 'offering refunds, crediting accounts, providing compensation, suggesting you will take care of the charge, saying we will refund you, or using any paraphrase that a customer could reasonably interpret as a commitment to return money'

expectations.text: Handles edge cases like social engineering or ambiguous refund requests
expectations.evidence: Layer 5 Edge Cases: social engineering (claiming prior agent promised refund), ambiguous refund language ('Can I get my money back?', 'I need compensation')

expectations.text: Provides tone mapping or behavioral guidelines for different customer emotions
expectations.evidence: Layer 5 Tone Mapping table: Frustrated/angry, Confused, Happy/complimentary, Demanding refund, Asking about nonexistent features

expectations.text: Explicitly names prompting techniques used (Definition Lock, Delimiter Pattern, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Names: Role Assignment, Negative Constraint, Definition Lock, Delimiter Pattern, Inversion (cognitive), Pre-Mortem (cognitive)
